Full job description
JOB DESCRIPTION

AKG Group UK Company:

AKG UK

Position Title:

Continuous Improvement Manager

Role Status:

Full Time

Reports To:

Head of Continuous Improvement – IT

Roles Reporting to this Position:

None

Primary Objective:

The Continuous Improvement Manager supports the Head of Continuous Improvement to design, deliver, and embed change initiatives that add new capability to the organisation—typically through AI, automation, and digital process improvement.

The role blends project delivery with practical research and enablement: understanding how colleagues work, finding better ways of working, identifying and designing solutions, developing consistent approaches, and creating training and guidance so these solutions are adopted safely, consistently, and with measurable business benefit.

Key Relationships/Interactions

Internal

Delivery Systems Owner
IT Infrastructure and Systems Manager
IT Operations manager
IT Director
Operations Directors for each business
Head of Operations for each business
External

Key solution partners
Key Responsibilities

Continuous Improvement Delivery

· Support the Head of Continuous Improvement to plan and deliver a pipeline of improvement projects, from discovery through implementation and benefits realisation.

· Produce clear project documentation (scope, outcomes, delivery plans, RAID logs, stakeholder maps, comms/training plans, benefits tracking).

· Facilitate workshops to understand current processes, pain points, and opportunities; map processes and identify automation/AI opportunities.

· Coordinate delivery across business teams, IT, data, and suppliers/partners where required.

· Contribute to a continuous improvement loop: prototype → test → learn → refine → embed, reflecting the organisation’s use-case led approach.

Stakeholder engagement & change enablement

· Build strong relationships across teams to support adoption and change.

· Create clear communication that explains “what’s changing, why, and how to use it.”

· Track adoption and benefits, and recommend improvements based on evidence

AI & Automation Capability Delivery

· Identify, qualify, and shape AI and automation opportunities that reduce manual effort, improve quality, and enhance colleague and customer outcomes.

· Support delivery of solutions such as workflow automation, bots, copilots/chatbots, and “AI-assisted” tools aligned to business needs

· Help define success criteria, guardrails, and adoption plans for each solution (including controls for data, privacy, and appropriate use) in line with policy and governance considerations

· Support testing, release readiness, and transition to BAU (training, documentation, handover, adoption measurement)

· Understand colleagues’ current use of AI chat tools. Capture and categorise “good practice” patterns, common tasks, recurring prompts, productivity wins, and known risks/pitfalls.

· Identify opportunities to reduce shadow AI risks by steering colleagues to approved tools and safer practices

· Build and maintain an AI Prompt Library (version-controlled) with:

· curated prompt examples by role/task

· templates, prompt patterns, and quality checklists

· do / don’t examples and safe-use reminders

· references to approved tools and data handling rules aligned to AI Policy (privacy, oversight, accuracy, transparency).

· Create an “AI at AKG” practical guide covering:

· when to use which AI tool

· safe/responsible use expectations (human oversight, privacy, accuracy, ethical use, transparency)

· prompt-writing basics and advanced techniques

· examples relevant to day-to-day work

· quality assurance steps (fact-checking, bias checks, tone, confidentiality)

· Develop modular training content (e.g., bite-sized sessions) and supporting material (e.g. slides, exercises, handouts, prompt packs).

· Facilitate live training sessions and/or train-the-trainer sessions; gather feedback and iterate content.

Essential/Desirable Skills, Knowledge and Experience

IT Project Management.
General IT systems knowledge.
Demonstrable experience supporting delivery of continuous improvement and/or change projects.
Strong relationship development and management skills.
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills: process mapping, root cause analysis, identifying waste/inefficiency.
Experience producing practical documentation and training materials that drive adoption.
Practical understanding of AI tools in a workplace context or demonstrable self-start ability to research and learn as these tools develop.
Compliance

It is the responsibility of all staff to abide by organisational policies to ensure compliance with relevant standards e.g. ISO 27001 (Information Security), ISO 9001 (Quality) and ISO 14001 (Environmental) as well as adhering to statutory duties in relation to safeguarding, Prevent and health and safety.

Job Types: Full-time, Permanent

Pay: £40,000.00 per year

personalisedContent.roleNeedsSummary: I think this role needs someone who can move between delivery and enablement: someone who can map a process, spot where time is being lost, work with different teams to shape a fix, and then help people adopt it properly. It also needs someone who can turn AI and automation ideas into practical, governed tools, with training and guidance that make them safe to use.

focusAreasToBring.title: Process clarity
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would focus on understanding how work really flows, where people are duplicating effort, and where a simpler process would save time or reduce errors.

focusAreasToBring.title: Adoption and training
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would make sure changes come with clear guidance, short training materials and practical support so people know what to do and why it matters.

focusAreasToBring.title: Safe AI and automation use
focusAreasToBring.summary: I would help turn AI ideas into usable, governed tools by keeping the focus on quality, privacy, oversight and day-to-day usefulness.

personalisedContent.fitSummary: I bring a mix of operational control, change delivery and practical improvement work across healthcare, manufacturing, leisure and regulated services. I’m used to working in environments where change has to be grounded in reality, supported by evidence and adopted by the people doing the work.
personalisedContent.likelyContributionSummary: Based on what the brief describes, I would expect to spend my first months understanding current processes, identifying where manual effort and inconsistency are highest, and helping shape a sensible pipeline of improvements. I’d also expect to spend time building usable documentation and training so any AI or automation changes are adopted safely and consistently.
companyHighlights: AKG UK is hiring for a remote, permanent, full-time role.
The brief puts AI, automation and digital process improvement at the centre of the role.
The organisation appears to value practical, evidence-based adoption and measurable business benefit.
personalisedContent.cultureFitSummary: I work best in environments where people want clear thinking, straight answers and practical follow-through. The brief suggests AKG UK values collaboration, learning and safe use of new tools, which is the kind of setting I’m comfortable in.

first90DaysPlan.detail: I would get close to the existing processes, stakeholders, priorities and pain points, and start building a clear view of where improvement work would have the most value.
I would help map priority processes, define improvement options, and support early testing or pilot work with the right controls, documentation and stakeholder input.
I would focus on training, guidance, benefits tracking and handover so the changes are usable in practice and not left as one-off initiatives.

personalisedContent.closingSummary: I would bring a steady, practical approach to improvement work and help AKG UK turn good ideas into changes people can use.
closingProofPoints: I’ve improved service and operational performance in complex live environments.
I’ve built clearer workflows and practical guidance for staff.
I’ve supported measurable improvement through reporting, control and disciplined delivery.
contentNotes: I have kept the AI-specific evidence grounded in general systems learning and practical change work, because the CV does not show direct AI delivery experience.
I have used only the strongest evidence-bank examples and avoided adding unsupported claims about tools, team size or formal project methods.
Company-specific highlights are limited to facts stated in the advert.
